sutta: monks i will define 8 excitable horses and the 8 vices 8 excitable men and the 8 vices listen well pay heed i will speak monks 1 of the 8 excitable horses and the 8 vices here in monks when an excitable horse is told to go on being beaten and urged by the driver he backs and twists the carriage round with his hind quarters such indeed herein is an excitable horse and this is his first vice tour to go on being beaten and urged by the driver he jumps back batters against the carriage railing and breaks the triple bar this is his second vice he loses his hindquarters from the pole and tramples on it this is his third vice he takes the wrong road and makes the carriage go awry that means go wrongly this is his fourth vice he tosses high his breast and pours the air this is his fifth vice heatless of the driver and the goat he champs the bid with his teeth and wanders at ram at random this is his sixth vice when urged by the driver he goes neither on nor back but halts and stands like a pose this is his seventh vice moreover months when an excitable horse is told to go on being beaten and urged by the driver he draws together his fore and hind legs and just sits down there on his 4 feet such indeed is an excitable horse and this is his eighth rise monks these are the 8 excitable horses and the 8 vices and what monks are the 8 excitable men and the 8 vices here in monks the monks reproved 1 of themselves for some offence and he being thus reproved evades the matter by a plea of forgetfulness saying i don't remember i don't remember this as an excitable horse when beaten and urged by his driver backs and twists the carriage round like that i say is this person such indeed hiring is an excitable man and this is his first vice or on being reproved he blurts out at his reprover what right have you to talk an ignorant fool why do you think you must be monks this is an excitable horse jumps back and batters the carriage railing back then i say is this person this is his second vice or he retorts well you 2 committed such and such an offense you had best make a man's verse monks this is an excitable horse loses his hind water from the pole like then i say is this person this is his third vice or he evades the question by another turns the issue aside and shows temper ill will and saltiness monks just as an excitable horse takes the wrong road and makes the carriage go awry like that i say is this person this is his fourth vice or he speaks when the sangha is in private meeting with much gesticulation monks this is an excitable horse tosses high his breasts and draws the air like that i say is this person this is his fifth vice or he pays no attention to the sangha nor heeds his reprover but wanders about at random like an offender monks just like an excitable horse is heatless of the driver and the goat and champs is big like then i say is this person this is his sixth lies all he says but i have not committed an offense no i have not offended and he waxes the sangha by his silence monks this is an excitable horse when urged goes neither on or back but just holds and stands like a pose like that i say is this person this is his seventh vice moreover monks when the monks reprove 1 of themselves for some offence he being thus reproved by them says reverend says worry so much about me from now on i will disavow the training and return to the lower life and when he has returned to the lower life he says now raven says be satisfied monks this is an excitable horse when told to go on being beaten and urged by the driver draws his fore and hind legs together and sits down like that i say is this person such indeed herein is an excitable man and this is his eighth vice monks these are the 8 excitable men and these are the 8 vices that's the end of the super just now we heard the comparison of a good manga with a good horse now we hear the comparison of a excitable horse and an excitable monk this excitable monk means his mind is very excitable he's got a brain and this kind this type of manga is no joy to live with so during the buddha's time there were many many types of monks just as we have now so the buddha has a lot of experience with them that's what he can mention all these types of monks and the next utah is
